pages.text:
MEMORANDUM OF UNDERSTANDING

BETWEEN

THE EMBASSY OF THE FRENCH REPUBLIC IN ADDIS ABABA

AND

THE EMBASSY OF THE CZECH REPUBLIC IN ADDIS ABABA

ON

THE JOINT EVACUATION PLAN

The Embassy of the French Republic in Addis Ababa and the Embassy of the Czech Republic in Addis

Ababa (hereinafter jointly referred to as "Parties" and individually as "Party"),

Expressing their will to closely cooperate in the evacuation of the French and Czechs citizens, have reached the following understanding:

Article 1

The objective of this Memorandum of Understanding (hereinafter referred to as "MoU"') is to provide a basis for the cooperation of the Parties on the evacuation of their citizens from Ethiopia in case such a need arises due to a severe crisis in the country.

Article 2

The Parties will cooperate in providing the assistance to all the citizens of France and the Czech Republic in Ethiopia who are in need of evacuation.

Article 3

1. The Embassy of France expresses its will and confirms its capability to provide a regrouping and evacuation support to all citizens of the Czech Republic who are not able to evacuate themselves individually or by the means of the Embassy of the Czech Republic.

2. Prior to a possible regrouping, the Embassy of the Czech Republic will be responsible for crisis communication towards its nationals and their dependents.

3. Once the regrouping is decided, the Embassy of the Czech Republic will assign an agent who will be in charge of the safety of the Czech community and who will serve as a link between the French

Embassy and will therefore pass on the guidelines of the Embassy of France to their community.

4. To manage the regrouping in the compound of the Embassy of France, The Embassy of the Czech

Republic will assign an agent who will be in charge of the consular aspects related to the Czech community.

1

5. The Embassy of the Czech Republic will be timely providing an updated list of its citizens living in

Ethiopia to enable the Embassy of France proper planning of the required evacuation capacity.

Article 6

The Parties will hold consultative meetings to discuss their cooperation as well as update each other on the modifications to their evacuation plans.

Article 7

This MoU confirms commitments and intentions of the parties and is not legally binding.

Article &

This MoU may be amended by mutual consent of the Parties through exchange of diplomatic notes.

Article 2

This MoU will enter into force upon signature and will remain valid for a period of two years, unless either party terminates it by giving three months prior notice in advance.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the undersigned representatives, have signed this MoU.

signed in Add Abat... on this 29' forder 2014, in two copies in English both being equally authentic.

FOR THE EMBASSY OF THE REPUBLIC

OF FRANCE IN ADDIS ABABA

FOR THE EMBASSY OF THE CZECH

REPUBLIC IN ADDIS ABABA

Batter

...-

12 Brigitte COLLET

Ambassadrice de France en Ethiopie
